api: Box Platform REST API (https://api.box.com/2.0), OpenAPI 3.0 spec at https://raw.githubusercontent.com/box/box-openapi/main/openapi.json
scope: Pass B full-surface expansion, scoped to account/enterprise-level catalog resources plus the connector-configured folder_id (matching this connector's actual spec.json config surface — a Box service-account app, not a file/folder-tree crawler): every one of the 294 non-file-content-management endpoints reachable without a caller-supplied specific file_id/folder_id beyond the one configured folder_id is enumerated below (this mirrors github's own precedent of narrowing api_surface.json to the endpoints a real config shape can reach, rather than every endpoint Box publishes). 11 read streams (users, groups, collections, folder_items, webhooks, retention_policies, legal_hold_policies, storage_policies, sign_requests, terms_of_services, metadata_templates, pending_collaborations — 12 total) and 9 write actions (group/webhook/collaboration create-update-delete) are implemented. The ~86 file/folder/web-link-item-scoped endpoints (content upload/download, per-file/folder metadata/comments/tasks/versions/shared-links/watermarks) require a specific file_id/folder_id/web_link_id this connector has no independent way to enumerate (folder_items lists ONE configured folder's immediate children, not a recursive tree), and are excluded as out_of_scope/binary_payload per-endpoint below alongside every other account-level endpoint not covered by a stream or write.
